revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:寳登山神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017, 09:12 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hodosan Shrine |image = File:Hodosan Shrine (Mt. Treasure-climb Shrine) - 宝登山神社 - panoramio (13).jpg |location = 1828 Nagatoro, {{ill|Nagatoro, Saitama|en|Nagatoro, Saitama|qid=Q1345299|lt=Nagatoro Town|1=Nagatoro, Saitama}}, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|qid=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} |ISO = JP-11 |Rank = 36|5|35.60 |longitude = 139|6|11.15 |deity = {{ill|Jimmu|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}; {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine
Special Shrine |founded = (Tradition) 41st year of Emperor Keikō's reign (111 AD) |main hall = [[Gongen-zukuri]] |alternate name = |Pilgrimage Sites = Chichibu Three Shrines |festival = {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}} (Main festival) |Rituals = Fire Purification ceremony (Monthly on 7th)
Okumiya festival ({{ill|May 2|en|May 2|qid=Q2545|lt=May 2|1=May 2}})
Funatama festival ({{ill|August 15|en|August 15|qid=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}}) |map = Japan Saitama |location = {{ill|Nagatoro|qid=Q1345299|1=Nagatoro, Saitama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.093222|N|139.103097|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} {{integrated character|瀞|[[File:Kanji for another OS version - jou.svg|20px]]}} '''Hodosan Shrine''' (宝登山神社, Hodosan Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Hodo|ja|宝登山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hodo|1=Mount Hodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452648}} in {{ill|Nagatoro, Saitama|en|Nagatoro, Saitama|qid=Q1345299|lt=Nagatoro Town|1=Nagatoro, Saitama}}, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|qid=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The shrine was formerly a prefectural shrine and is currently listed as a special shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the {{ill|Chichibu Three Shrines|ja|秩父三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550808}}, along with {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|qid=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine|1=Chichibu Shrine}} and {{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}}. The Okumiya (rear shrine) is located at the summit of Mount Hodo. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}''' (Kan'yamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祗神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Ōyama-tsumi no Kami) * '''{{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}''' (Homusubi no Kami) == History == According to shrine tradition, in the 41st year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign (111 AD), the Emperor's son, {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, while on an expedition to the east, prayed at the mountain summit. A giant dog appeared and guided him to the summit. Along the way, they encountered a fierce fire from the northeast. The dog bravely jumped into the fire, extinguishing it, allowing Prince Yamato Takeru to safely reach the summit and pray. The prince, grateful for the dog's help, enshrined the deity of fire protection, Kagutsuchi, along with Ōyamatsumi, the god of the mountain, at the mountain base. This event is considered the origin of the shrine. In May 2011, the shrine was awarded one star in the French edition of the "Michelin Green Guide Japon." The {{ill|Aioi no Matsu|ja|相生の松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aioi no Matsu|1=Aioi no Matsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582262}} (Aioi Pine) is believed to bring good fortune in love. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Hōgyoku Inari Shrine * Fujiya Fuchi Shrine * Japan Warrior Prince Shrine * Tenman Tenjin Shrine * Shōkon Shrine * Water Shrine == festivals == * {{ill|January|en|January|qid=Q108|lt=January|1=January}} **{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}・New Year's Luck Praying festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}}) :: * {{ill|February|en|February|qid=Q109|lt=February|1=February}} **National Foundation Day Celebration ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|qid=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}) * {{ill|March|en|March|qid=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} **Industrial Praying festival ({{ill|March 1|en|March 1|qid=Q2393|lt=March 1|1=March 1}}) * {{ill|April|en|April|qid=Q118|lt=April|1=April}} **Main festival ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}) * {{ill|May|en|May|qid=Q119|lt=May|1=May}} **Okumiya festival ({{ill|May 2|en|May 2|qid=Q2545|lt=May 2|1=May 2}}) * {{ill|July|en|July|qid=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} **Yasaka Great festival (Gion festival) ({{ill|July 20|en|July 20|qid=Q2728|lt=July 20|1=July 20}}) * {{ill|August|en|August|qid=Q122|lt=August|1=August}} **Funatama festival ({{ill|August 15|en|August 15|qid=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}}) * {{ill|November|en|November|qid=Q125|lt=November|1=November}} **Industrial Thanksgiving festival ({{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}) ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}) * {{ill|December|en|December|qid=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} **Year-End festival ({{ill|December 31|en|December 31|qid=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}}) == location Information == ; location * 1828 Nagatoro, {{ill|Nagatoro, Saitama|en|Nagatoro, Saitama|qid=Q1345299|lt=Nagatoro Town|1=Nagatoro, Saitama}}, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|qid=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} ; Access {{underline|Train}} * Nearest Station: {{ill|Nagatoro Station|en|Nagatoro Station|qid=Q8013090|lt=Nagatoro Station|1=Nagatoro Station}} on the {{ill|Chichibu Main Line|ja|秩父本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Main Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秩父鉄道秩父本線|1=Chichibu Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5096033}}, {{ill|Chichibu Railway|en|Chichibu Railway|qid=Q843780|lt=Chichibu Railway|1=Chichibu Railway}} (approximately 8 minutes on foot) {{underline|Car}} * From {{ill|Hanazono Interchange|ja|花園インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanazono Interchange|1=Hanazono interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11615194}} on the {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 140|en|Japan National Route 140|qid=Q6156790|lt=National Route 140|1=Japan National Route 140}} for about 20 minutes {{underline|To the Okumiya (Mount Hodo summit)}} * Take the {{ill|Hodo Ropeway|ja|宝登山ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hodo Ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝登興業|1=Hodosan Ropeway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5876274}} from the foot station to the summit station, then walk approximately 5 minutes from the summit station '''Nearby''' * Gyokusenji Temple - founded in the first year of Eikyū (1113 AD){{sfn|New Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Fujiyabuchi Village|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿 藤谷淵村}}'s former betto temple == See Also == * {{ill|Chichibu Three Shrines|ja|秩父三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550808}} ** {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|qid=Q2963366|lt=Chichibu Shrine|1=Chichibu Shrine}} ** {{ill|Mitsumine Shrine|ja|三峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsumine Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mount Hodo|ja|宝登山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hodo|1=Mount Hodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452648}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|title={{ill|New Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Musashi Fudoki Manuscript|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}|volume=Volume 251, Chichibu District 7|chapter=Fujiyabuchi Village, Gyokusenji Temple|id={{NDLJP|764013/75}}|publisher=Geography Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Fujiyabuchi Village|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿 藤谷淵村}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hodosan-jinja}} * [https://www.hodosan-jinja.or.jp/ Hodosan Shrine] (official site) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]s}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[ja:寳登山神社]] {{translated page|ja|寳登山神社|version=104706345|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11452649}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
